text: The play of neon blue light against the glass and the geometric lines tilts my perception of reality; I feel as if I have crossed a threshold between the ordinary and the abstract. There is an anticipation in the air, as though I am about to witness or become part of some experiment in perception or thought. The presence of glass walls makes me aware of boundaries—both literal and intellectual. I feel curious, slightly disoriented, and open to the encounter with the unknown that this space invites.
